3GPP_Reference.Title: NG-RAN; F1 Application Protocol (F1AP); TS 38.401 NG-RAN; Architecture Description

Feature_Description: This procedure is used for the case when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation for LTM (L1/L2 Triggered Mobility).

The UE Context Setup procedure is initiated by the gNB-CU to establish the UE Context, including SRB, DRB, BH RLC channel, Uu Relay RLC channel, PC5 Relay RLC channel, and SL DRB configuration, in the candidate gNB-DU for the purpose of Inter-gNB-DU LTM handover.
Feature_Objectives: To establish UE Context in a candidate gNB-DU for Inter-gNB-DU LTM handover.
To configure LTM (L1/L2 Triggered Mobility) for the UE in the candidate gNB-DU.
To provide CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM to the candidate gNB-DU.

To enable the gNB-CU to request lower layer configuration from the candidate gNB-DU for generating the LTM reference configuration, or to provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU.
To request early TA acquisition (early UL synchronisation) information from the candidate gNB-DU.
To receive generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell from the candidate gNB-DU.
Feature_Implementation_Steps: 1. The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration based on L3 measurement reports from the UE.
2. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell. This message contains:
- The CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM.
- Optionally, the LTM configuration ID mapping list.
- Optionally, a request for PRACH resources.

- Optionally, a request for the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for generating the reference configuration, or the gNB-CU may provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration.

3. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message. This message includes the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 1. The gNB-CU initiates the procedure by sending UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU.

2. If the LTM Indicator IE set to "true" is contained in the LTM Information Setup IE included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, the gNB-DU shall consider that the request concerns LTM for the included SpCell ID IE.

3. If the Request for Lower Layer Configuration IE set to "true" is contained within the Reference Configuration IE in the LTM Information Setup IE, the gNB-DU shall provide the lower layer configuration in the Reference Configuration Information IE in the LTM Configuration IE in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message.

4. If the Reference Configuration Information IE is contained within the Reference Configuration IE in the LTM Information Setup IE, the gNB-DU shall take it into account for generating the LTM lower layer configuration.

5. If the CSI Resource Configuration IE is contained in the LTM Information Setup IE, the gNB-DU shall use it to generate the LTM CSI reporting configuration(s) in the CellGroupConfig IE for the requested LTM candidate cell.
6. If the LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE is contained, the gNB-DU shall consider this as the mapping information for the LTM candidate cell(s).

7. If the Early Sync Information Request IE is included, the gNB-DU shall include the Early Sync Information IE of the accepted candidate cell for early TA acquisition (early UL synchronisation) in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message.
8. If the gNB-DU succeeds to establish the UE context, it replies to the gNB-CU with UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE.
9. The UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message shall include the received SpCell ID IE as the Requested Target Cell ID IE.

10. If the Early UL Sync Configuration IE is included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message, the gNB-CU shall consider it as the generated early UL sync information from the accepted candidate cell.

11. If the Early UL Sync Configuration for SUL IE is included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message, the gNB-CU shall consider it as the generated early UL sync information for SUL from the accepted candidate cell.
12. If the LTM Configuration IE is included in the UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message, the gNB-CU shall consider it as the generated configuration for LTM from the accepted candidate cell.

13. If the Complete Candidate Configuration Indicator IE set to "complete" is contained in the LTM Configuration IE, the gNB-CU shall consider that the LTM candidate configuration is a complete candidate configuration.

Information_Elements.IE_Name: LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE
Request for Lower Layer Configuration IE
Early UL Sync Configuration for SUL IE

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: The UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message may include generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell. Specifically, the CellGroupConfig IE, which contains RRC configurations, can be transparently signaled to the UE via RRC messages. The LTM Configuration IE in the response contains these generated lower layer RRC configurations.
